title: Application Sign-In with Mandatory MFA
description:
## Authenticating Employees in a Web App

An internal web application must authenticate company employees and enforce **mandatory multi-factor authentication**. The business requires support for **TOTP authenticator apps** (e.g., Google Authenticator) and, as a fallback, **SMS one-time codes**. The team wants an AWS-native, managed identity service for **application end-user sign-in** that handles user directories, password policies, and MFA without building custom auth. Which is the BEST approach?

options.text: Create an IAM user for each employee and require IAM MFA at sign-in
Use an Amazon Cognito user pool with MFA set to required, enabling TOTP and SMS factors (SMS delivered via Amazon SNS)
Build a custom authentication service storing password hashes in DynamoDB and generate your own TOTP/SMS codes
Issue static API keys to each employee and validate them at the application's API Gateway

explanation:
An **Amazon Cognito user pool** is AWS's managed identity provider for **application end users**. It provides a hosted user directory, password policies, sign-up/sign-in flows, and **MFA that can be set to *required***, supporting **software token (TOTP)** authenticator apps and **SMS** one-time codes. SMS codes are delivered through **Amazon SNS** — in production you must move the account **out of the SNS SMS sandbox** and set an SMS **spend limit**, and AWS now recommends TOTP as the primary factor with SMS as fallback.

(a) **IAM users are not meant for application end-user login** — IAM identities are for accessing the AWS API/console and management plane. Creating an IAM user per employee to log into your app is an anti-pattern: it doesn't scale, mixes app auth with AWS account access, and isn't designed for app sign-in flows. (c) **Building custom auth** reinvents password storage, MFA, and token issuance — high effort and high risk versus a managed service. (d) **Static API keys** are not user authentication and provide no MFA, no user identity, and no rotation of a human credential.

hints.title: App users vs. AWS users
hints.body: IAM identities authenticate to AWS itself (console/API). Application end users belong in a Cognito user pool.

hints.title: MFA factors and SMS plumbing
hints.body: Cognito user pools support required MFA with TOTP and SMS; the SMS path uses Amazon SNS and requires leaving the SMS sandbox in production.
